methodsAttempted.notes: No Firecrawl-specific tool was exposed in this isolated worker session, so discovery used official-domain public web search and direct HTTP fetches.

methodsAttempted.query: site:umbc.edu generative AI policy UMBC students faculty ChatGPT
methodsAttempted.notes: Search surfaced official UMBC DoIT AI pages, the UMBC AI resources page, academic integrity resources, course-specific pages, and news/event items.

methodsAttempted.query: doit.umbc.edu AI GenAI Tools Administrative AI Academic and Instructional AI
methodsAttempted.notes: The DoIT AI navigation exposed GenAI Tools, Administrative AI, Academic and Instructional AI, Research AI, and AI Support pages. Three DoIT pages had direct, source-specific policy or guidance evidence.

Verified doit.umbc.edu, ai.umbc.edu, academicconduct.umbc.edu, calt.umbc.edu, and my3.my.umbc.edu as UMBC-controlled official subdomains. Robots.txt allowed the fetched public paths for the generic user agent except restricted administrative paths.

artifacts.summary: Official UMBC discovery found three current DoIT AI guidance pages with substantive claims about approved/supported GenAI tools, data handling, administrative AI safeguards, teaching expectations, and AI detection. A cached FDC academic-integrity URL returned 404 on direct fetch and a myUMBC event item was rejected as event-only guidance.

artifacts.claimText: UMBC's DoIT GenAI Tools guidance says UMBC content that is not public should not be used with a GenAI service unless UMBC has verified that the service is safe to use, and says UMBC has several tools verified as safe for Level 1 and FERPA data.
artifacts.normalizedValue: non-public UMBC content requires UMBC-verified safe GenAI service; several tools verified for Level 1 and FERPA data

artifacts.sourceTitle: GenAI Tools - Division of Information Technology - UMBC
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Unless this information is considered public material, something you would publish on a website for the Internet to see, you should not use content from UMBC on any GenAI service unless you know that UMBC has verified it is safe to use. Luckily, UMBC has access to several GenAI tools that have been verified as safe to use on UMBC Level 1 & FERPA data, which is data intended to be kept internal to UMBC.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Unless UMBC content is public, DoIT says it should not be used with a GenAI service unless UMBC has verified the service is safe; it also says several GenAI tools are verified for Level 1 and FERPA data.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Set clear expectations for using generative AI tools including how to cite or reference appropriately, if permitted (Mollick & Mollick, 2023). artifacts.sourceTitle: Academic and Instructional AI - Division of Information Technology - UMBC
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: There are a number of AI detection tools available, some built on older APIs for ChatGPT and therefore they are not accurate just on this factor alone. AI, by its nature, is constantly learning and improving itself and it may never be possible to truly detect whether text is AI-generated. Note: UMBC does not license any AI detection tools at this time.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: UMBC notes reliability limitations of AI-detection tools and states that it does not license any AI detection tools at this time.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.title: University of Maryland, Baltimore County AI Policy Staged Evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged UMBC evidence from official DoIT AI pages supports five agent-reviewed candidate claims covering GenAI tool verification and data handling, administrative AI safeguards, supported campus AI tools, teaching expectations for generative AI use, and AI-detection-tool status.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are staged OpenClaw candidates only and are not marked human reviewed or canonical.
This run did not use cached-only or event-only pages as claim evidence.
